{{Quote|I thought it might do us some good if Luke yells something defiant while that brute is choking the life out of him. We've nothing to lose by it. Luke doesn't either. The Coway admire spirit.|[[Halla]] to [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]]|<ref name="Splinter of the Mind's Eye">''[[Splinter of the Mind's Eye]]''</ref>}}
The '''Coway''' were a primitive race of [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]] that were native to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Mimban/Legends|Mimban]]. Because of their intense dislike of outsiders, they spent most of their lives in the caves beneath the planet's fog-shrouded swamps.<ref name="Guide to the Star Wars Universe (Coway)">''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe]]''.</ref>

==Biology and appearance==
[[File:Coway2.png|thumb|left|200px|A Coway warrior.]]
Coway were bipedal and covered with a fine red down. They had relatively large eyelids and small eyes which were capable of seeing into the infrared spectrum, which they used to survive in the subterranean darkness. A Coway's diet typically consisted of fruits, [[Lichen/Legends|lichens]], raw meats and fungus which would be poisonous to them if not for the strong amino acids of his or her digestive system.<ref name="Essential Guide to Alien Species" />

==Society and culture==
Compared to the space-age cultures of the rest of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], the Coway were a primitive lot. Their main technological achievements were fire<ref name="Splinter of the Mind's Eye" /> and weapons made of [[flowstone]].<ref name="Splinter of the Mind's Eye" /> They had little to no sense of higher learning or technology<ref name="Essential Guide to Alien Species" /> and were fond of charms.<ref name="Guide to the Star Wars Universe (Coway)" /> They treated death as an inevitable, everyday occurrence.<ref name="Splinter of the Mind's Eye" />

Coway tribes lived in large villages where order was maintained by brute force. Any outsiders who ventured too close would be attacked. Males were dominant in Coway society and each village was ruled by a triumvirate of chiefs who answered only to the will of [[Canu]], a warrior god.<ref name="Essential Guide to Alien Species" />

If the chiefs encountered gridlock when they attempted to resolve an issue by themselves, they submitted the problem to Canu. Because the Coway believed that Canu demanded strength from his followers,<ref name="Essential Guide to Alien Species" /> the greatest warriors of the conflicting parties would face each other in single combat until one quit or died. The winner of the contest would be presumed to be favored by Canu and therefore the chiefs would rule in favor of the party that the winner fought for. If the loser had lost because he quit, he and the party that he championed would abide by Canu's judgment and pay his respects to him by hitting the winner in the face. The winner was expected to return the favor or else face the prospect of angering Canu—and another physical contest.<ref name="Splinter of the Mind's Eye" />

The largest known Coway village contained a population of at least two hundred. It was located in a large subterranean amphitheater near an ancient [[Thrella]] city that was adjacent to a subterranean lake.<ref name="Encyclopedia">''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> The village itself contained two Thrella structures that the tribe kept in an outward state of repair at the very least.<ref name="Splinter of the Mind's Eye" />

==History==
The cave drawings of the Coway and the pictographs of the [[Mimbanite]]s—another intelligent species native to Mimban and genetic cousins of the Coway— were extremely similar to those found in the [[Temple of Pomojema]]. This led many [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[Archaeology/Legends|xenoarchaeologists]] to theorize that the two species and the temple—as well as the ancient civilization that had built it—were connected in some way.<ref name="Essential Guide to Alien Species" />

The Coway commonly made their way to the surface through [[Coway shaft]]s which connected to [[Thrella well]]s as well as sinkholes and other openings.<ref name="Splinter of the Mind's Eye" /> During their early days, they could come and go as they pleased.<ref name="Splinter of the Mind's Eye" /> However, when the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] discovered that Mimban was rich in [[Dolovite/Legends|dolovite]], it established a mining facility on the planet. Rather than be subjugated by the Empire as their genetic cousins would be, the Coway retreated further into the subterranean depths, believing that they could find some refuge from the Empire's [[energy drill]]s.<ref name="Essential Guide to Alien Species" />

[[File:Coway_triumvirate.jpg|thumb|The leaders of a Coway tribe decide the fate of a group of interlopers.]]

The Coways' relative peace was short-lived. The Empire's mining operations managed to further disrupt their way of life. The Coway responded by rebelling with occasional acts of unpredictable violence.<ref name="Essential Guide to Alien Species" /> Sometime after the Imperial presence had been well-established, Coway would only periodically venture into the outskirts of the five mining towns. If they were confronted, they would flee. This behavior led many of the locals and miners to mistakenly believe that the Coway were shy and peaceful.<ref name="Splinter of the Mind's Eye" />
